ops.text: fli (pypi "flights[mcp]") can never return HND-OKA results: its Airport enum keys members by IATA code but values them by airport name, and OKA (Naha, Okinawa) shares the value "Naha Airport" with NAH (Tahuna, Indonesia), so Python's enum makes Airport["OKA"] an alias of Airport.NAH and every OKA query is silently sent to Google as NAH — zero flights on every date, no error. The reverse decode also fails: _AIRPORT_BY_CODE in fli/search/_decoders.py is built from canonical members only, so rows Google returns with "OKA" raise AttributeError and are skipped. Workaround that works: patch FlightSearchFilters.encode to .replace("NAH","OKA") on the encoded payload and add _decoders._AIRPORT_BY_CODE["OKA"] = Airport["NAH"] before searching. Any getaway cash-hop pricing to OKA (a stock Tokyo→Okinawa positioning leg) via fli hits this until fixed upstream.
